issues.title: SLA target is asserted without a defensible composite availability model
issues.description: The artifact marks 99.5% SLA as achievable, but the selected stack includes SWA Free and a single-instance App Service B1 path. Reliability validation does not show a composite SLA calculation or per-component SLA assumptions, so the stated SLA objective is not evidence-backed.
issues.location: ## Requirements Validation; ## Reliability Assessment; ## Architecture Decision Summary
issues.recommendation: Add an explicit composite availability calculation using documented SLA terms for each selected SKU/tier and deployment topology. If 99.5% cannot be supported with current tiers/topology, either adjust the NFR for dev or change the architecture/SKUs accordingly.

issues.title: Availability Zone upgrade path is internally inconsistent
issues.description: Reliability recommendations state 'upgrade to S1+ App Service Plan to enable Availability Zones', while the SKU comparison table indicates S1 has no AZ support. This creates a faulty reliability evolution path.
issues.location: ## Reliability Assessment; ## Resource SKU Recommendations (App Service tier comparison)
issues.recommendation: Correct the resilience roadmap to a topology/SKU that actually supports zonal resilience (P1v3+), or remove AZ claims for this phase. Document the exact tier and deployment pattern required to meet each future reliability target.

issues.title: RTO 24h is not backed by a dependency-ordered recovery procedure
issues.description: The artifact relies on manual Terraform re-apply as interim DR, but does not define ordered restoration across state backend, SQL restore point selection, app config/secret dependencies, and app validation checkpoints.
issues.recommendation: Add a stepwise recovery runbook with dependency order, decision points, expected durations, and validation gates.

issues.title: RPO claim depends on SQL backup behavior that is not explicitly specified
issues.description: The architecture cites SQL PITR as the RPO mechanism, but does not specify backup storage redundancy or restore-region assumptions. In a regional failure scenario, RPO/RTO outcomes vary materially based on backup redundancy choices.
issues.recommendation: Explicitly declare SQL backup redundancy settings and the restore-region procedure used for DR assumptions.

issues.title: Cross-region frontend/backend split failure modes are not analyzed
issues.description: SWA is placed in westeurope while API/SQL are in swedencentral. The architecture does not model partial-outage behavior (frontend up, backend down), cross-region latency impacts, or degradation mode.
issues.recommendation: Add explicit failure-mode analysis for regional partition and backend outage scenarios, including graceful degradation behavior and incident routing triggers.

issues.title: Scale-out path lacks validated trigger-to-action playbooks
issues.description: Scale triggers are listed (e.g., >80 users, DTU >80%), but no validated capacity test data or pre-approved migration playbooks are included.
issues.recommendation: Define trigger-to-action runbooks with pre-tested SKU transitions, expected lead times, and rollback criteria.

summary: The architecture is cost-efficient for dev, but reliability assertions are currently stronger than the evidence provided. The largest risks are unproven SLA feasibility and an inconsistent AZ upgrade path; secondary risks are untested DR execution details, unspecified backup resilience assumptions, and unmodeled cross-region partial-failure behavior.
